findings.location: Key Concepts — Normalisation and its practical payoff (line 87)
findings.detail: The opening sentence of this paragraph runs to ~55 words and introduces scikit-learn, Keras, and Pinecone with inline parenthetical definitions all at once. Each parenthetical is a brief label rather than a taught concept, so this does not technically constitute a jargon_dump (no 3+ new terms requiring re-use), but the sentence is harder to parse than the 20-word target. Recommend splitting into two sentences in revision.

beginner_readability.notes: Terms are defined on first use: 'magnitude sensitivity' (with corpus pointer), 'theta (θ)' (with plain-language gloss), 'cosine' (with explicit 'you do not need trigonometry' reassurance), 'normalisation' (defined twice — in Key Concepts and Key Takeaways). Concrete examples precede abstractions throughout. No hedging language detected. No assessment references (A2, 15%, due W6) appear in the learner-facing prose. One soft finding (dense_paragraph) logged; total soft findings = 1, below the hard-fail threshold of 3.

faithfulness.notes: 10 assertions sampled across all sections and verified against topic_corpus.md: (1) dot product magnitude sensitivity problem — corpus §5.1; (2) dividing by magnitudes removes size sensitivity — corpus §5.3; (3) score range −1 to 1 — corpus §5.3; (4) worked example A=[3,1] B=[2,2], dot=8, |A|≈3.162, |B|≈2.828, cosine≈0.894 — corpus §5.3 exact match; (5) cat/kitten/car example with cosine 0.96 and 0.60 — corpus §6; (6) normalise once → subsequent comparisons become plain dot products — corpus §5.4; (7) king−man+woman nearest-word lookup uses cosine — corpus §7; (8) score 0 means unrelated not opposite — corpus §8; (9) many embeddings all-positive → practical range 0–1 — corpus §8; (10) cosine similarity is not a distance metric — corpus §8. All 10 assertions are supported. 0 unsupported assertions found.

verdict: PASS — all 6 checks passed; 1 soft readability finding (dense normalisation paragraph, line 87) logged but below escalation threshold.
